commonIssues:
Confusion over where to register the Will (residence vs property location).

How to fix:

  • A Will can be registered at any Sub-Registrar Office in TN, but it's best done near the testator's residence. Check TNREGINET for SRO details.

The testator must be present, which is hard for ill or immobile individuals.

How to fix:

  • Apply for a Private Attendance via TNREGINET, where the Sub-Registrar will visit the testator's home or hospital for an additional fee.

Delays in Patta transfer and confusion over Probate requirements in Chennai.

How to fix:

  • As per the recent amendment, probate is no longer strictly mandatory in Chennai, but Revenue Records (Patta) must be updated swiftly with the registered Will and Death Certificate.

Many believe a registered Will cannot be challenged, which is false.

How to fix:

  • Ensure the execution process is bulletproof: attach a medical certificate, use trusted witnesses, and avoid beneficiary involvement in the drafting process.

Heirs are often unaware of the registered Will's existence.

How to fix:

  • Consider depositing the Will with the District Registrar for safe custody or inform a trusted executor about its location.
